intro.0: My research focuses on improving the safety, security, and reliability of artificial intelligence systems. In my latest work, I focused on studying how to identify datasets used in the training of large language models and develop fairer methods for evaluating their capabilities.
intro.1: As physical agents increasingly leverage foundation models for perception, reasoning, and decision-making, my research focuses on developing reliable, robust, and safe frameworks for integrating these models into real-world autonomous systems.
intro.2: A key challenge in this domain is the limited availability of data covering the full spectrum of environmental conditions and adversarial behaviors encountered in the real world. My research therefore investigates the robustness of these systems under out-of-distribution and adversarial scenarios, with the goal of developing secure, aligned, and trustworthy algorithms for physical AI.
